name: Edgar Barrera
description:
'''Edgar Barrera''' (born April 18, 1990), also known by his professional name '''Edge''', is an American [[songwriter]], [[Record producer|producer]], and musician based in [[Miami]], Florida. He has won a total of 29 [[Latin Grammy Awards]] and 1 [[Grammy Awards|Grammy Award]] including "Producer of The Year" in 2021, 2023, and 2024; and "Songwriter of The Year" in 2023, 2024, and 2025. As a songwriter he has won a total of 44 BMI Awards, making him one of the most awarded writers in [[Latin music]]. He has written and produced songs for artists such as [[Ariana Grande]], [[Camila Cabello]], [[Ed Sheeran]], [[Madonna]], [[Selena Gomez]], [[Peso Pluma]], [[Karol G]], [[Bad Bunny]], [[Grupo Frontera]], [[Maluma]], [[Christian Nodal]], [[Manuel Turizo]], [[Rels B]], [[The Weeknd]], [[Shakira]], [[Camilo (singer)|Camilo]], [[Grupo Firme]], [[Fuerza Regida]], [[Carín León]], [[Marc Anthony]], and [[Natanael Cano]].

At the [[13th Annual Latin Grammy Awards|13th]], [[14th Annual Latin Grammy Awards|14th]], [[16th Annual Latin Grammy Awards|16th]], [[22nd Annual Latin Grammy Awards|22nd]], [[24th Annual Latin Grammy Awards|24th]], [[25th Annual Latin Grammy Awards|25th]], and [[26th Annual Latin Grammy Awards|26th]] Latin Grammy Awards ceremonies, Barrera made headlines for being one of the musicians with the most nominations during these years, achieving the feat of being the most nominated musician overall in 2023 and 2024. Barrera received a nomination for the [[Grammy Award for Songwriter of the Year, Non-Classical]] at the [[66th Annual Grammy Awards|66th]] and [[67th Annual Grammy Awards]].
